problem.title: IMU-DR (pure strapdown INS) aiding ablation on the NCLT 2013-01-10 120-frame window
problem.question: IMU-DR is a pure strapdown IMU-only dead-reckoning baseline (no LiDAR, no GT seed) added for family context alongside OdoNet/NHC-Net/NN-ZUPT. How much does each cheap aid (ZUPT, midpoint vs Euler integration, static-init gyro-bias estimation) change drift on a 24s NCLT ground-vehicle window? Expected to drift badly relative to LiDAR/LIO methods -- this documents the baseline honestly, not a tuned result.

variants.label: Default (pure strapdown DR, midpoint, no ZUPT)
variants.intent: Repository default: 2.0s static-init gyro-bias estimation, midpoint integration, ZUPT off. ATE 9.071 m / RPE 170.6%/100m on the 11.5 m / ~24 s window -- unaided IMU dead reckoning drifts to roughly 80% of the trajectory length in RMSE, as expected for a baseline with no external aiding.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=1 zupt=0 zupt_frames=0; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Uses the default CLI surface only.
variants.extensibility_note: No extra profile knobs beyond the stable core contract.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: + ZUPT
variants.intent: Enable zero-velocity updates. ATE 9.071 -> 2.887 m (-68%), RPE 170.6% -> 30.6%/100m (-82%). zupt_frames=481/1051 IMU samples inside the integration window were gated as stationary -- this short window has the vehicle at rest for a large fraction of the time, so ZUPT is the single most effective aid tested here. Do not read this as representative of a continuously-moving trajectory.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=1 zupt=1 zupt_frames=481; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Best combined benchmark score on the shared dataset and interface.

variants.label: Forward Euler (vs default midpoint)
variants.intent: Swap midpoint integration for forward Euler, all else default. ATE 9.071 -> 10.280 m (+13%), RPE 170.6% -> 198.9%/100m (+17%). Midpoint is mildly better as expected, but the gap is small next to ZUPT/gyro-bias -- integration scheme is a second-order effect at ~5 Hz effective IMU rate over 24 s.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=0 zupt=0 zupt_frames=0; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: No static-init gyro-bias estimation
variants.intent: Disable the 2.0s static-init gyro-bias estimate, all else default. ATE 9.071 -> 24.676 m (+172%), RPE 170.6% -> 482.0%/100m (+183%) -- by far the largest degradation of the three ablations. Uncorrected gyro bias integrates into heading error which then aliases into large position drift; static-init bias estimation is the most load-bearing aid of the three, more so than ZUPT on this window.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=1 zupt=0 zupt_frames=0; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.
